title: Add or Subtract Mixed Numbers by Parts
description: Add or subtract mixed numbers by operating on whole-number parts and fractional parts (after ensuring common denominators).
condition: Two mixed numbers with like denominators or already rewritten with a common denominator are being added or subtracted.
response: Add or subtract the whole numbers and the fractional parts separately, then combine into a single mixed number.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires prior creation of equivalent fractions when denominators differ.

title: Compare Fraction to 1
description: Determine whether a fraction is less than, equal to, or greater than 1 by comparing numerator and denominator.
condition: Fraction a/b with positive integers a and b.
response: State that the fraction is <1 if a<b, =1 if a=b, and >1 if a>b.
label: Principle / Model
example: Since \(\frac{5}{4}\) has numerator greater than denominator, \(\frac{5}{4} > 1\).

title: Compare Fractional Value to Given Benchmark
description: Determine whether a fraction or mixed number is greater than a specified benchmark value.
condition: Given a fraction or mixed number and a benchmark value (e.g., $1\tfrac{1}{2}$).
response: Determine if the fraction is greater than the benchmark by comparing values (using common denominators, number line, or reasoning).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Used to decide which data points exceed a threshold on the line plot.

title: Compare Product to Original Using Fraction Size
description: Determine whether a product is greater than, equal to, or less than the original number based on whether the fraction multiplier is greater than 1, equal to 1, or less than 1.
condition: Expression of the form a × b where a is a positive number and b is a fraction; need to compare a × b to a.
response: If b > 1, conclude a × b > a; if b = 1, conclude a × b = a; if 0 < b < 1, conclude a × b < a.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Assumes positive whole numbers and positive fractions.

title: Convert Kilometers to Meters
description: Convert a measurement from kilometers to meters by multiplying by 1,000.
response: Multiply by 1,000 to express the measurement in meters.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses 1 km = 1,000 m and place value reasoning.

title: Convert Kilometers to Millimeters
description: Convert kilometers to millimeters using metric place-value relationships.
condition: Given 1 kilometer and asked for number of millimeters.
response: Use 1 km = 1000 m and 1 m = 1000 mm to compute 1,000,000 mm (10^6).
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Meters to Centimeters
description: Convert a measurement from meters to centimeters by multiplying by 100.
response: Multiply by 100 to express the measurement in centimeters.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Milliliters to Liters
description: Convert a quantity in milliliters to liters using the factor 1,000 milliliters per liter.
response: Divide the number of milliliters by 1,000 to express in liters.
label: Principle / Model

title: Count Data Points Meeting a Condition
description: Count the number of data points on a line plot that satisfy a specified condition.
condition: A line plot is given and a condition such as "greater than $1\tfrac{1}{2}$" is specified.
response: Count all marks on the line plot whose values satisfy the condition.
label: Production / Skill
example: If six X's lie to the right of $1\tfrac{1}{2}$ on the number line, then the answer is 6 days.
notes: Requires accurate reading of tick marks and fractional intervals.

title: Generate Equivalent Fraction by Multiplying by 1
description: Create an equivalent fraction by multiplying numerator and denominator by the same nonzero number.
response: Compute (a×n)/(b×n) to form an equivalent fraction.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Equivalent Numerical Representations
description: Determine which expressions represent the same numerical value.
condition: Given multiple numerical expressions and a target number.
response: Select all expressions that are equal in value to the target number.
label: Principle / Model
example: Since $10^8 = 100{,}000{,}000$ and 100 million = 100,000,000, both are equivalent representations.
notes: Requires evaluating and comparing forms (expanded, word form, exponential).

title: Identify Greatest and Least Values on Line Plot
description: Determine the maximum and minimum data values represented on a line plot.
condition: A line plot displays data points along a number line.
response: Locate the leftmost (least) and rightmost (greatest) data points and state their values.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the leftmost X is at $\tfrac{3}{8}$ and the rightmost X is at $2\tfrac{7}{8}$, these are the least and greatest values.
notes: Precursor step to finding the difference between extremes.

title: Interpret Fractional Scale on Number Line
description: Recognize and use fractional subdivisions (e.g., eighths) between labeled whole numbers on a number line.
condition: Number line with whole numbers labeled and equal fractional tick marks between them.
response: Determine the fractional value represented by each tick (e.g., each tick is 1/8).
label: Principle / Model
example: If there are 8 equal intervals between 3 and 4, each tick represents \(\frac{1}{8}\).

title: Interpret Word Form with Place Value Units
description: Convert between word form using units (e.g., million, thousand) and standard numerical form.
condition: Given a number written in word form with place value units (e.g., '100 million').
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Relies on knowledge that 1 million = 1,000,000 (conventional place value naming).

title: Subtract Fractions to Find Difference
description: Compute the difference between two fractions or mixed numbers to determine how much greater one value is than another.
condition: Two fractional quantities are compared and the question asks how much more or the difference between them.
response: Subtract the smaller quantity from the larger using common denominators and simplify if needed.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes identifying which quantity is greater before subtracting.
